quality.rationale: 基于Cartan旋转1-形式法求解一般球对称时空的正交标架联络系数，需多步微分形式外微分与分量比对，逻辑链条严密且具有明确的动力学天体物理背景。

origin.question:
Compute the connection coefficients of the following metric in an orthonormal frame
$$
\begin{array}{l} \mathrm {d s}^{2} = - \mathrm {e}^{2 \alpha} \mathrm {d t}^{2} + \mathrm {e}^{2 \beta} \mathrm {d r}^{2} + \mathrm {e}^{2 \gamma} (\mathrm {d}\theta^{2} + \sin^{2} \theta \mathrm {d} \phi^{2}) \\ a, \beta , \gamma = f u n c t i o n s o f r a n d t. \\ \end{array}
$$
origin.answer:
$$\begin{array}{r l} \Gamma_{\widehat {r t}}^{\widehat {t}} & = \Gamma_{\widehat {t t}}^{\widehat {r}} = a^{\prime} e^{- \beta} \end{array}$$
$$\Gamma^{\hat {\mathbf {t}}}_{\mathbf {\hat {r r}}} = \Gamma^{\hat {\mathbf {r}}}_{\mathbf {\hat {t r}}} = \dot {\beta} \mathrm {e}^{- a}$$
$$\Gamma^{\hat {\mathbf {t}}}_{\hat {\boldsymbol {\theta}}} \hat {\boldsymbol {\theta}} = \Gamma_{\hat {\mathbf {t}}}^{\hat {\boldsymbol {\theta}}}_{\hat {\boldsymbol {\theta}}} = \Gamma_{\hat {\boldsymbol {\phi}}}^{\hat {\mathbf {t}}}_{\hat {\boldsymbol {\phi}}} = \Gamma_{\hat {\mathbf {t}}}^{\hat {\boldsymbol {\phi}}}_{\hat {\mathbf {t}}} = \dot {\gamma} \mathbf {e}^{- \alpha}$$
$$\Gamma^{\hat {\mathbf {r}}}_{\hat {\theta}} \hat {\theta} = - \Gamma_{\hat {\mathbf {r}}}^{\hat {\theta}}_{\hat {\theta}} = \Gamma^{\hat {\mathbf {r}}}_{\hat {\phi}} \hat {\phi} = - \Gamma^{\hat {\phi}}_{\hat {\mathbf {r}}} \hat {\phi} = - \gamma^{\prime} \mathrm {e}^{- \beta}$$
$$\Gamma^{\hat {\theta}}_{\hat {\phi}} \hat {\phi} = - \Gamma^{\hat {\phi}}_{\hat {\theta}} \hat {\phi} = - \cot \theta e^{- \gamma}.$$
origin.solution:
Solution 8.27. The difficult way to do this problem is to compute connection coefficients in a coordinate frame. Here we will use the method of "rotation 1-forms." (See MTW, Section 14.5 for details.) Choose an orthonormal frame as follows:
$$
\begin{array}{l} \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\mathbf {t}}} = \mathbf {e}^{\alpha} \tilde {\mathbf {d t}} \\ \tilde {\mathbf {w}}^{\hat {\mathbf {r}}} = \mathrm {e}^{\beta \tilde {\mathbf {d r}}} \\ \tilde {\mathbf {w}}^{\hat {\theta}} = \mathbf {e}^{\gamma} \tilde {\mathbf {d}\theta} \\ \tilde {\omega}^{\hat {\phi}} = e^{\gamma} \sin \theta \widetilde {d \phi}. \\ \end{array}
$$
The key formulas are the definition of $	ilde{\omega}^{\hat{a}}\hat{ eta}$
$$
\mathbf {d} \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\alpha}} = - \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\alpha}} \hat {\boldsymbol {\beta}} \wedge \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\beta}},
$$
and the relation of the forms to the Christoffel symbols
$$
\Gamma_{\hat {\mu} \hat {\lambda}}^{\hat {\nu}} \tilde {\pmb {\omega}}^{\hat {\lambda}} = \tilde {\pmb {\omega}}_{\hat {\mu}}^{\hat {\nu}}.
$$
We now use these formulas for the given metric; let dot denote $\partial/\partial t$ and prime denote $\partial/\partial r$ :
$$
\mathrm {d} \tilde {\omega}^{\hat {\mathrm {t}}} = \mathrm {e}^{\alpha_{\alpha^{\prime}} \widetilde {\mathrm {d r}} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d t}}} = \alpha^{\prime} \mathrm {e}^{- \beta \tilde {\omega}^{\hat {\mathrm {r}}} \wedge \tilde {\omega}^{\hat {\mathrm {t}}}} 	ag {1}
$$
$$
\mathrm {d} \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\mathbf {r}}} = \mathrm {e}^{\beta} \dot {\boldsymbol {\beta}} \widetilde {\mathrm {d t}} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d r}} = \mathrm {e}^{- \alpha} \dot {\boldsymbol {\beta}} \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\mathbf {t}}} \wedge \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\mathbf {r}}} 	ag {2}
$$
$$
\begin{array}{l} \mathrm {d} \tilde {\omega}^{\hat {\theta}} = \mathrm {e}^{\gamma} \dot {\gamma} \widetilde {\mathrm {d t}} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d}\theta} + \mathrm {e}^{\gamma} \gamma^{\prime} \widetilde {\mathrm {d r}} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d}\theta} \\ = e^{- \alpha} \dot {\gamma} \tilde {\omega}^{\hat {t}} \wedge \tilde {\omega}^{\hat {\theta}} + e^{- \beta} \gamma^{\prime} \tilde {\omega}^{\hat {r}} \wedge \tilde {\omega}^{\hat {\theta}} 	ag {3} \\ \end{array}
$$
$$
\begin{array}{l} \mathrm {d} \tilde {\omega}^{\hat {\phi}} = \mathrm {e}^{\gamma} \dot {\gamma} \sin \theta \widetilde {\mathrm {d t}} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d}} \phi + \mathrm {e}^{\gamma} \gamma^{\prime} \sin \theta \widetilde {\mathrm {d r}} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d}} \phi + \mathrm {e}^{\gamma} \cos \theta \widetilde {\mathrm {d}\theta} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d}} \phi \\ = e^{- \alpha} \dot {\gamma} \tilde {\omega}^{\hat {t}} \wedge \tilde {\omega}^{\hat {\phi}} + e^{- \beta} \gamma^{\prime} \tilde {\omega}^{\hat {r}} \wedge \tilde {\omega}^{\hat {\phi}} + \cot \theta e^{- \gamma} \tilde {\omega}^{\hat {\theta}} \wedge \tilde {\omega}^{\hat {\phi}} 	ag {4} \\ \end{array}
$$
But we also know that $\mathbf{d}	ilde{\pmb{\omega}}^{\hat{\alpha}} = -	ilde{\pmb{\omega}}^{\hat{\alpha}}\hat{ eta} \wedge 	ilde{\pmb{\omega}}^{\hat{ eta}}$ and $	ilde{\pmb{\omega}}_{\hat{\alpha}}\hat{ eta} = -	ilde{\pmb{\omega}}_{\hat{ eta}}\hat{\alpha}$ , where indices are raised and lowered with $\eta_{\hat{\alpha}}\hat{ eta}$ . For $\hat{\alpha} = \hat{t}$ we have
$$
\mathbf {d} \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\mathbf {t}}} = - \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}_{\hat {\mathbf {r}}}^{\hat {\mathbf {t}}} \wedge \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\mathbf {r}}} - \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}_{\hat {\boldsymbol {\theta}}}^{\hat {\mathbf {t}}} \wedge \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\boldsymbol {\theta}}} - \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}_{\hat {\boldsymbol {\phi}}}^{\hat {\mathbf {t}}} \wedge \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\boldsymbol {\phi}}} .
$$
Comparing this with Equation (1), we guess that
$$
\tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}_{\hat {\mathrm {r}}}^{\hat {\mathrm {t}}} = \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}_{\hat {\mathrm {t}}}^{\hat {\mathrm {r}}} = \alpha^{\prime} \mathrm {e}^{- \beta} \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\mathrm {t}}} + \mathrm {K}_{1} \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\mathrm {r}}} 	ag {5}
$$
$$
\tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\mathrm {t}}_{\hat {\theta}} = \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\theta}}_{\hat {\mathrm {t}}} =. \mathrm {K}_{2} \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\theta}} 	ag {6}
$$
$$
\tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}_{\hat {\phi}}^{\hat {\mathrm {t}}} = \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}_{\hat {\mathrm {t}}}^{\hat {\phi}} = \mathrm {K}_{3} \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\phi}} 	ag {7}
$$
where $\mathbf{K}_1, \mathbf{K}_2, \mathbf{K}_3$ are functions we must find. Similarly, Equation (2) implies that
$$
\tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}_{\hat {\mathbf {t}}}^{\hat {\mathbf {r}}} = \mathbf {e}^{- \alpha} \dot {\boldsymbol {\beta}} \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}_{\hat {\mathbf {r}}}^{\hat {\mathbf {r}}} + \mathrm {K}_{4} \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\mathbf {t}}} 	ag {8}
$$
$$
\tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\mathbf {r}}}_{\hat {\theta}} = - \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\theta}}_{\hat {\mathbf {r}}} = \mathrm {K}_{5} \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\theta}} 	ag {9}
$$
$$
\tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\mathbf {r}}} \hat {\phi} = - \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\phi}}_{\hat {\mathbf {r}}} = \mathrm {K}_{6} \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\phi}}. 	ag {10}
$$
Equation (3) implies
$$
\tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}_{\hat {\mathrm {t}}}^{\hat {\theta}} = \mathrm {e}^{- a} \dot {\gamma} \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\theta}} 	ag {11}
$$
$$
\tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}_{\hat {\mathbf {r}}}^{\hat {\theta}} = \mathrm {e}^{- \beta} \gamma^{\prime} \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\theta}} 	ag {12}
$$
$$
\tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\theta}}_{\hat {\phi}} = - \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\phi}}_{\hat {\theta}} = \mathrm {K}_{7} \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\phi}}, 	ag {13},
$$
and Equation (4) gives
$$
\tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}} \hat {\phi}_{\hat {\mathbf {t}}} = e^{- a} \dot {\gamma} \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}} \hat {\phi} 	ag {14}
$$
$$
\tilde {\omega}^{\hat {\phi}}_{\hat {\mathrm {r}}} = \mathrm {e}^{- \beta} \gamma^{\prime} \tilde {\omega}^{\hat {\phi}} 	ag {15}
$$
$$
\tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\phi}}_{\hat {\theta}} = \cot \theta \mathrm {e}^{- \gamma} \dot {\tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}}^{\hat {\phi}}. 	ag {16}
$$
Comparing Equations (5)-(16), we find
$$
\tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}_{\hat {\mathbf {r}}}^{\hat {\mathbf {t}}} = \alpha^{\prime} \mathrm {e}^{- \beta} \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\mathbf {t}}} + \dot {\beta} \mathrm {e}^{- \alpha} \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\mathbf {r}}}
$$
$$
\tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\mathrm {t}}} \hat {\theta} = \mathrm {e}^{- a} \dot {\gamma} \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\theta}}
$$
$$
\tilde {\pmb {\omega}}^{\hat {\mathrm {t}}} \hat {\pmb {\phi}} = \mathbf {e}^{- a} \dot {\gamma} \tilde {\pmb {\omega}}^{\hat {\phi}}
$$
$$
\tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\mathbf {r}}}_{\hat {\boldsymbol {\theta}}} = - \mathrm {e}^{- \beta} \gamma^{\prime} \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\boldsymbol {\theta}}}
$$
$$
\tilde {\pmb {\omega}}^{\hat {\mathbf {r}}} \hat {\phi} = - \mathrm {e}^{- \beta} \gamma^{\prime} \tilde {\pmb {\omega}}^{\hat {\phi}}
$$
$$
\tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\boldsymbol {\theta}}}_{\hat {\boldsymbol {\phi}}} = - \cot \theta \mathrm {e}^{- \gamma} \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\boldsymbol {\phi}}} .
$$
3ut $\Gamma_{\hat{\mu}\hat{\lambda}}^{\hat{
u}}	ilde{\omega}^{\hat{\lambda}} = 	ilde{\omega}_{\hat{\mu}}^{\hat{
u}}$ so the nonvanishing connection coefficients are
$$
\begin{array}{r l} \Gamma_{\widehat {r t}}^{\widehat {t}} & = \Gamma_{\widehat {t t}}^{\widehat {r}} = a^{\prime} e^{- \beta} \end{array}
$$
$$
\Gamma^{\hat {\mathbf {t}}}_{\mathbf {\hat {r r}}} = \Gamma^{\hat {\mathbf {r}}}_{\mathbf {\hat {t r}}} = \dot {\beta} \mathrm {e}^{- a}
$$
$$
\Gamma^{\hat {\mathbf {t}}}_{\hat {\boldsymbol {\theta}}} \hat {\boldsymbol {\theta}} = \Gamma_{\hat {\mathbf {t}}}^{\hat {\boldsymbol {\theta}}}_{\hat {\boldsymbol {\theta}}} = \Gamma_{\hat {\boldsymbol {\phi}}}^{\hat {\mathbf {t}}}_{\hat {\boldsymbol {\phi}}} = \Gamma_{\hat {\mathbf {t}}}^{\hat {\boldsymbol {\phi}}}_{\hat {\mathbf {t}}} = \dot {\gamma} \mathbf {e}^{- \alpha}
$$
$$
\Gamma^{\hat {\mathbf {r}}}_{\hat {\theta}} \hat {\theta} = - \Gamma_{\hat {\mathbf {r}}}^{\hat {\theta}}_{\hat {\theta}} = \Gamma^{\hat {\mathbf {r}}}_{\hat {\phi}} \hat {\phi} = - \Gamma^{\hat {\phi}}_{\hat {\mathbf {r}}} \hat {\phi} = - \gamma^{\prime} \mathrm {e}^{- \beta}
$$
$$
\Gamma^{\hat {\theta}}_{\hat {\phi}} \hat {\phi} = - \Gamma^{\hat {\phi}}_{\hat {\theta}} \hat {\phi} = - \cot \theta e^{- \gamma}.
$$
